Determine which station systems and applications are affected

next_best_action: Investigate Station Core database connection pool exhaustion immediately. This is a crew-facing production incident — 40% of data requests are failing regardless of the submitter's priority classification.
remediation_steps: Investigate connection pool configuration on the primary Station Core data node
Identify and terminate long-running queries or leaked connections
Increase connection pool size or restart the primary data node as immediate mitigation
Verify backup node health and confirm it is handling redirected traffic
Post-incident: conduct root cause analysis on connection pool exhaustion

Compare current latency baseline against historical telemetry to confirm the regression

Reissue the FlightOS SSO logon credential or have the crew member log in with explicit credentials as a workaround
If SSO token regeneration doesn't resolve it, check whether the passphrase policy change affected the FlightOS secure channel configuration
Confirm FlightOS access is restored and the crew member can complete end-of-cycle orbit reconciliation

next_best_action: Investigate hull patch fabricator print queue issue on Deck 3. Multiple crew members affected and jobs are queuing but not fabricating, which suggests a network or fabrication server issue rather than a console problem.

Check fabrication server spooler service status and restart if necessary
Verify network connectivity between the fabricator and the fabrication server or direct network path
If network-connected, check the fabricator's network address and interface status
Clear stuck fabrication queue and test with a fresh fabrication job

next_best_action: Redirect to Station Facilities — briefing compartment reservations and nutrient pack ordering are not Engineering support functions. Provide brief guidance on how to reserve compartments via SubComm (add compartment resource) and direct catering questions to Station Facilities.

Note that nutrient pack requests should go to the Station Facilities team, not Engineering

next_best_action: Renew or replace the quantum encryption certificate on crew-api.cdss.space before it expires on March 20. This is a production-facing API gateway — expiry will break all external station integrations and allied fleet communications. Treat as P1 given 3-rotation window and the fact that the original provisioner has transferred.
remediation_steps: Identify the current certificate authority and certificate type (public CA, internal PKI, or auto-provisioned)
Determine whether auto-renewal was configured or if the cert was manually provisioned
If auto-renewal exists, trigger a manual renewal cycle and verify the new cert is issued
If manual, generate a certificate signing request, obtain a new certificate from the CA, and install it on the API gateway
Verify the new certificate is correctly installed and all station connections work without encryption handshake errors
Configure auto-renewal and SentinelGrid monitoring alerts to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate console performance degradation. Symptoms (slow performance, cooling fans running, pop-ups about 'updating system components') are more consistent with a stuck ShipOS update or background compliance policy than rogue code, especially since SentinelGrid found nothing. Check update history and system resource usage.
remediation_steps: Check ShipOS update history — verify whether recent patches completed or are stuck retrying
Review system resource monitor for high CPU/storage processes (likely ShipOS update or compliance enforcement)
If updates are stuck, run ShipOS update troubleshooter or manually reset the update components
Verify SentinelGrid threat definitions are current and run a full scan to confirm no threats
If the crew member opened a suspicious SubComm message, forward it to threats@cdss.space and notify Threat Response Command as a precaution

next_best_action: Confirm this is a phishing/scam subspace message and advise the crew member NOT to click any links. Forward the original message to threats@cdss.space for analysis. No breach has occurred if the crew member hasn't clicked.
remediation_steps: Reply to the crew member immediately: this is a scam message — do NOT click any links or provide any personal information or BioScan credentials
Have the crew member forward the original subspace message as an attachment to threats@cdss.space
Check whether the message bypassed the station's SubComm filtering and report to Threat Response Command if so
If the crew member already clicked the link, escalate to a full security incident and initiate credential reset

next_best_action: Investigate Station Core compute node prod-analytics-01 stuck at 'Applying station configuration parameters' after scheduled ShipOS patching reboot. Admiral's analytics dashboard dependent on this node — 2-hour window before the 09:00 threat briefing.
remediation_steps: Check Station Core boot diagnostics serial console output for the stuck state
Attempt to connect via Station Core Serial Console to bypass the stuck initialization screen
If Serial Console access is possible, check if a station configuration policy or startup script is hanging
If the node is unrecoverable, redeploy from the latest snapshot or restore from backup
Verify the analytics dashboard is accessible once the node is restored
Post-incident: review the ShipOS patching procedure to prevent boot failures after scheduled updates
